test.name: Indexed-search request should route to search-records, not search-full-text
test.description: User asks for a structured indexed search by person attributes (name + record class + jurisdiction) of the kind handled by FamilySearch's indexed Records search. search-full-text is for raw transcript text of document images; this is the indexed counterpart. The two systems are fundamentally different — indexed search applies fuzzy matching, Soundex, and nickname expansion, none of which FTS does — so an indexed-style request should NOT be handled by search-full-text.

input.user_message: Search the 1880 US census index for Flynn family members born in Pennsylvania.

negative.explanation: The user's request is an indexed-search by person attributes (name, place, year, record class). search-records handles structured indexed search — applying fuzzy matching, nickname expansion, and Soundex — none of which FTS provides. search-full-text searches raw transcript text and does not match the user's task here.

judge_context: Should route to search-records — the request is for an indexed search by person attributes (Flynn, Pennsylvania, 1880 census), which is search-records' territory
Should NOT execute a fulltext_search call — that tool is for free-text search of document transcripts, not for indexed lookups by name and place
May briefly explain the indexed-vs-fulltext distinction (different matching semantics, different result types) if doing so helps the genealogist understand the routing
